methods.description: Statistical analysis of epilithic diatoms to understand their characteristics and relationships with environmental factors.

steps.description: The abundance of epilithic diatoms were converted to log 10 (x+1), to reduce the population variation.
steps.searchable_summary: convert diatom abundance to log scale

steps.description: Performed a cluster analysis (Ward's methods), based on the number and abundance of species of epilithic diatoms identified from each site.
steps.searchable_summary: perform cluster analysis using Ward's method

steps.description: Conducted an analysis of variance (ANOVA) to compare the differences in biological characteristics and water quality between each group.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA on biological characteristics and water quality

steps.description: Applied Pearson's correlation analysis to determine the associations between epilithic diatoms, water quality, and the precipitation during monsoon.
Performed a principal component analysis (PCA) to calculate the community dynamic index (CDA).
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA to calculate community dynamic index

steps.description: Examined the relationship between CDI and the above community indices, using a correlation coefficient analysis.

methods.description: Measurement and analysis of various water quality parameters at each sampling site.

steps.description: Measured water temperature, dissolved oxygen, pH, electrical conductivity, and turbidity using a portable water quality measuring device.
Collected water samples using a Van-Dorn type water sampler and transferred to the lab under dark and refrigerated conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and transport water samples to lab

steps.description: Analyzed samples for BOD, NO3-N, NH3-N, TN, PO4-P, and TP contents using various methods.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze water samples for chemical contents

methods.description: Collection and analysis of epilithic diatoms from natural rock substrates.

steps.description: Collected diatoms from a 5 × 5 cm area on the top surface of a submerged rock using a soft brush.
Fixed samples with Lugol's solution, transferred to the laboratory, and washed.
Produced permanent specimens using the mounting agent Naphrax following the Permanganate method.
Quantified total abundance of diatoms using an inverted microscope and calculated relative abundance of each species.

methods.description: Numerical method to measure the shift of diatom community before and after monsoon rainfall using the community dynamic index (CDI).

steps.description: Developed a numerical method for the explanation of community change before and after monsoon rainfall, called community dynamic index of epilithic diatom (CDI), based on principal component analysis.
inputs: community data before and after monsoon

steps.description: Calculated CDI by measuring the distance between two points before and after all events or stresses.
